claim: XLA (Accelerated Linear Algebra) is a domain-specific ML compiler for CPU/GPU/TPU that lowers framework graphs (JAX/TensorFlow/PyTorch) from StableHLO (a portable, versioned, MLIR-based op set) to HLO (functional SSA IR), runs optimization passes, and codegens via LLVM (CPU x86/ARM; GPU NVPTX->PTX) with Triton emitters for matmul/softmax fusions; XLA is the only compilation path for TPU

claim: Fusion is XLA's single most important optimization: it groups operations into one GPU kernel with the invariant that no intermediate is materialized in HBM (passed via registers/shared memory), driven by PriorityFusion (cost-model greedy) and Multi-Output fusion; XLA:GPU also groups thunks into a CommandBufferThunk that records a GPU command buffer once and replays it to avoid per-kernel CPU launch overhead
queries: XLA fusion no intermediate HBM one kernel PriorityFusion Multi-Output
